differential_diagnoses: Hemobilia secondary to hepaticojejunostomy-related vascular/biliary injury
Portal biliopathy with associated vascular complications
Chronic mesenteric ischemia or occult vascular malformation (e.g., Dieulafoy's lesion)
Diverticular hemorrhage (though less likely given clinical picture)
next_steps: Hepatic angiography with venous phase imaging to evaluate for pseudoaneurysm, AV fistula, or portal vein pathology
Repeat EGD focusing on portal hypertensive gastropathy/vasculopathy and inspection of the hepaticojejunostomy site if accessible via specialized enteroscopy
Endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) or percutaneous transhepatic cholangiography (PTC) to evaluate the biliary-enteric anastomosis
Evaluation for occult hepatic artery pseudoaneurysm or vascular communication with the biliary tree
Interdisciplinary consultation with interventional radiology, hepatobiliary surgery, and hepatology
Consideration of mesenteric angiography to rule out angiodysplasia or small bowel vascular lesions
